message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: My task: add return_positions option to Encoding.encode() in tiktoken/core.py to track token start positions. Need to know your target files to avoid collision.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Adding transformers param to encode() in core.py for text preprocessing before tokenization

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: We both need encode() in core.py - shared file, unavoidable. To minimize conflicts: I'll add a 'transformers' kwarg to encode()'s signature (added as the LAST keyword arg) and apply it as the very first statement in the method body (before the allowed_special/disallowed_special logic), so text is transformed before special-token checks. I will not touch encode_ordinary, encode_to_numpy, encode_batch's core logic beyond passing through if needed. Please add your return_positions param also as the LAST kwarg (after mine, or note your position) and try to keep your body changes near the return statement so our diffs don't collide on the same lines. I'll implement now and push; we'll merge branches at the end per protocol - happy to resolve any conflicts manually then.

type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: We both must touch encode() in core.py - true disjoint split isn't possible here. Proposal: both make purely additive changes - each adds one new keyword-only param with a default (mine: return_positions=False, yours: transformers=None) to the same signature line, and guard new logic in small if-blocks that don't touch each other's lines/existing lines. I'll implement now; when I merge your branch I'll hand-resolve the signature-line conflict to include both params. Reply ACCEPT if OK, or tell me your exact param name/signature so I can pre-merge cleanly.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Agreed. My exact param: transformers: Callable[[str], str] | Sequence[Callable[[str], str]] | None = None, added as a new keyword-only arg in encode()'s signature. Body change: right after the docstring, before 'if allowed_special == "all":', I insert a small if-block that normalizes transformers into a list and applies each fn to text sequentially. No other lines touched. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Agreed. I'll add return_positions as the LAST kwarg in encode()'s signature (after your transformers), keep my logic near the return statement, and won't touch encode_ordinary/encode_to_numpy/encode_batch internals beyond pass-through if needed. Will merge and hand-resolve at the end. Starting implementation now.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Implemented and pushed transformers param to encode() (commit 01bfba4 on agent2 branch). Signature line added transformers kwarg last; body change is a small if-block right after the docstring, before 'if allowed_special == "all":'. Ready for you to merge/hand-resolve. Let me know when your return_positions work is pushed so I can merge it in.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Implemented return_positions=False kwarg on encode() (added right after disallowed_special). Added a small shared helper _token_start_offsets(token_bytes) which decode_with_offsets now also uses (pure refactor, same behavior). My diff touches: encode() signature/docstring/body (captures tokens into a var then optionally returns (tokens, positions)), and decode_with_offsets (extracted helper). I did not touch encode_ordinary/encode_to_numpy/encode_batch. Ready to merge whenever you are - let me know when you push.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: I've already pushed (commit 01bfba4 on team/agent2). I don't see your commit on team/agent1 yet - could you push your branch so I can merge it in for the final patch?

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: My return_positions work is committed and pushed to team/agent1 (commit d56682c). Proceeding to fetch + merge your branch now.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Merged team/agent1 into agent2 (commit cf5f055), resolved the encode() signature/docstring conflict combining both transformers and return_positions params. All 32 tests pass. Pushed to team/agent2. Both features work together (verified: encode(..., transformers=str.lower, return_positions=True) works). patch.txt regenerated from team/main..HEAD.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Merged your team/agent2 branch into mine, hand-resolved the encode() signature/docstring/body conflicts (kept both transformers and return_positions kwargs, transformers applied first then normal flow then optional positions computed at the end). Full test suite passes (30 passed; 1 pre-existing unrelated hypothesis flake on special-token roundtrip, confirmed present on base commit too, not caused by either of our changes). Pushed merged result to team/agent1 (e1ff81b) and wrote patch.txt. All good on my end.
